ogTitle: Chaelundi vs Chain Valley Bay: Climate Safety

heading: Chaelundi vs Chain Valley Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chaelundi, New South Wales and Chain Valley Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chaelundi has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Chain Valley Bay (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chaelundi or Chain Valley Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chaelundi has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Chain Valley Bay scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chaelundi scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Chain Valley Bay scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chaelundi or Chain Valley Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chaelundi has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
